title: Unused Method Parameters Should Be Removed
description: Parameters that are declared in a function but not used within the function's body can lead to confusion and potential errors in your code. The function's behavior remains the same disregarding the values passed to such parameters, but the programmer's intention won't be clearly expressed anymore. Therefore, removing function parameters that are not being utilized is considered best practice.
recommendation: Remove all unused parameters from the function. For exceptions like parameters that are annotated with @javax.enterprise.event.Observes, in overrides and implementation methods, interface default methods, non-private methods that only throw or that have empty bodies, and in overridable methods if the parameter is documented with a proper javadoc, the rule will not raise issues.
impact: Removing unused method parameters enhances the readability and maintainability of the code by reducing confusion and potential errors.

references.title: Unused Method Parameters Should Be Removed
